name: Jef D. Boeke
title: Professor
affiliations_raw: Institute for Systems Genetics, Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Pharmacology, Department of Biomedical Engineering
lab_synopsis: Leads a synthetic genomics and genome writing program focused on designing, assembling, and installing very large DNA constructs to interrogate genome regulation and build faithful disease models. Develops genome writing pipelines that combine yeast based assembly of multi segment loci with targeted delivery into yeast and mouse embryonic stem cells using strategies such as Big IN and mSwAP In. Uses these capabilities to build tailored mammalian disease models, including a genomically rewritten TAF1 humanization model of X linked dystonia parkinsonism that links intronic SVA elements to transcriptional defects and neurodegeneration. Applies synthetic locus designs such as reversed human HPRT1 to define default genome states and to probe transcriptional and chromatin activity across episomal and integrated contexts in yeast and mouse. Builds and automates big DNA assembly workflows, including Assemblatron and ultra low background yeast vectors, enabling scalable assembly and verification of 50 kb and larger constructs. Also explores how synthetic genome architecture shapes nuclear organization and gene regulation, including manipulation of the 3D organization of large synthetic yeast chromosomes.
common_experiments_raw:
Yeast based assembly of large DNA payloads in yeast assembly vectors (YAVs) and verification by sequencing
Landing pad installation and payload replacement using Cre mediated cassette exchange (RMCE) or CRISPR Cas9 based mSwAP In and related approaches
High throughput and automated big DNA assembly workflows using JIB or SURE vectors and oligo pool derived linkers (OLIGARCHY)
Genotyping and structural validation using targeted capture sequencing (Capture seq) plus whole genome sequencing when needed
Functional readouts including ATAC seq, RNA seq, CAGE seq, CUT and RUN and Hi C depending on the question
Preclinical genome editing based disease modeling in mouse embryonic stem cells including TAF1 hybrid humanized alleles and CRISPRa rescue strategies
research_keywords: Automation and robotics for DNA assembly
Jack In the Box vector system
experimental_capabilities: Large DNA assembly in yeast (assemblons, synthetic loci)
YAV based payload shuttling and delivery
Landing pad engineering in yeast and mammalian genomes
Cre mediated cassette exchange (RMCE)
CRISPR Cas9 integration and rewriting including mSwAP In
CRISPRa perturbation and rescue experiments
Capture seq targeted resequencing for integration verification
ATAC seq, RNA seq, CAGE seq, CUT and RUN epigenomic profiling
Hi C chromatin conformation assays
Long read sequencing for assembly validation (for example PacBio)
Automation for assembly and clone handling (colony picking, acoustic dispensing, liquid handling)
Yeast based assembly of 50 kb and larger DNA constructs using homologous recombination
Design and construction of assemblons and synthetic loci from multi segment builds
Automated big DNA assembly workflows using Assemblatron
Ultra low background yeast vector systems for assembly selection such as Jack In the Box and SURE
Targeted large payload installation in mouse embryonic stem cells using Big IN style strategies
Iterative genome rewriting using mSwAP In
Landing pad engineering and cassette exchange using Cre lox RMCE
CRISPR Cas9 editing and CRISPRa perturbation and rescue experiments
Integration and structural verification using Capture seq and targeted sequencing
Transcriptome profiling using RNA seq and CAGE seq
Chromatin and regulatory profiling using ATAC seq and histone mark mapping
Chromatin conformation assays including Hi C
Disease model generation and phenotyping in mouse including neuronal lineage targeting
Long read sequencing for assembly validation
model_systems: Large human loci including HPRT1 and TAF1
Neuronal lineage targeting for neurodegeneration models

publications.title: A genomically rewritten and tailored TAF1 mouse model for X-Linked Dystonia-Parkinsonism
Manipulating the 3D organization of the largest synthetic yeast chromosome
Synthetic reversed sequences reveal default genomic states
Assemblatron: automated yeast DNA assembly at scale

keywords: Automation and robotics for DNA assembly
Jack In the Box vector system
